nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; generally found on open sand and rubble substrates near reefs, typically along deeper seaward slopes between roughly 16 and 170 m, though individuals are reported to rise to about 15 m while feeding

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on zooplankton and small benthic invertebrates in the wild. In aquariums, generally accepts meaty items including vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, chopped fresh seafood, and over time often takes quality flake or pellet foods. Small frequent feedings tend to be best for this active perching predator.

summary: Despite the trade name, the Sandhopper Blenny is actually a sandperch (Parapercis schauinslandii), an active Indo-Pacific bottom-dweller with red-and-white vertical stripes. Generally considered reef-safe but tends to be territorial toward other bottom-dwellers and is a notorious jumper, so a tightly sealed lid is strongly advised.
careNotes: Note on identity: the aquarium trade name 'Sandhopper Blenny' is misleading — the species is a sandperch in the family Pinguipedidae, not a true blenny (Blenniidae). LiveAquaria and several other retailers list it as 'Parapercis sp.', but the red-and-white vertically striped morph commonly sold under this name has been identified as Parapercis schauinslandii (the Redspotted Sandperch / Lyretail Grubfish). A closely related species, Parapercis snyderi (U-mark Sandperch), is occasionally sold under the same trade name and has very similar husbandry needs. Tends to occupy the bottom of the aquarium, perching on rocks and sand and darting between vantage points to watch tankmates. Generally considered reef-safe with corals, but small shrimp and very small fish can be predated. Commonly described as territorial toward other bottom-dwellers (other sandperches, blennies, gobies, hawkfish) and is best housed singly unless the aquarium is large enough to support a mated pair. A tightly fitting lid is strongly recommended as this species has been widely reported to jump. Adult size shows wide cross-source disagreement: aquarium retailers typically state 4–5 inches (10–13 cm), while FishBase records 18.0 cm TL as the maximum for P. schauinslandii — the 10–18 cm range here brackets both. Minimum tank size also varies: most retailers cite a 30-gallon (~113 L) minimum, while That Pet Place advises 65 gallons (~246 L) given the species' active perching behavior, jumping tendency, and need for territory; 150 L is used here as a cautious middle ground that gives the fish adequate swimming space and a long footprint over depth. Water parameters were averaged across LiveAquaria, Quality Marine, That Pet Place, Tank Facts, and FishBase wild temperature data (22.4–28.8 °C).

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Listed as 'Parapercis sp.'; species confirmed as Parapercis schauinslandii via cross-reference with Live Gem Factory and That Pet Place which match the red-and-white striped morphology described.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Originally assigned secondary URL (https://www.fishbase.se/summary/Petroscirtes-breviceps.html) did not match the species LiveAquaria actually sells — Petroscirtes breviceps is a fang-blenny mimic, not the Sandhopper Blenny. Swapped to the FishBase page for Parapercis schauinslandii. Cross-checked taxonomy (Pinguipedidae, Perciformes), max size (18.0 cm TL), wild temperature range (22.4–28.8 °C), Indo-Pacific distribution, and zooplanktivorous diet.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Lists the Sandhopper Blenny as Parapercis snyderi (a closely related congener with very similar husbandry). Cross-confirmed water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), 30-gallon minimum tank, semi-aggressive/territorial temperament, reef-safe, carnivorous diet.

additional.name: That Pet Place
additional.notes: Confirmed Parapercis schauinslandii identity. Reports a larger minimum tank (65 gallons / ~246 L) reflecting the species' active perching behavior and territorial needs, max size 7 inches (~17.8 cm), 75–82°F, pH 8.0–8.4, SG 1.020–1.024, reef-safe but predates small fish and crustaceans.

additional.name: Tank Facts
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Pinguipedidae), max size 4 inches (~10 cm), 76–80°F, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026, bottom-dwelling Western Pacific origin (Japan and Philippines).

additional.name: Live Gem Factory
additional.notes: Independent confirmation of Parapercis schauinslandii identity (under the Sandhopper Blenny / Red Stripe Sand Perch trade names). Cross-checked semi-aggressive temperament, 30-gallon minimum, 5-inch max, carnivorous diet, reef-safe.
